task_completion.description: Open and read the network inventory file to retrieve camera details.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano) or terminal window displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Documents/network_inventory.txt', showing an IP address and credentials.

task_completion.description: Open the 'Add Device' dialog in the Nx Witness client.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Nx Witness application is open with the 'Add Device' or 'Add Camera' dialog box visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Enter the IP address and credentials to add the camera.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Add Device' dialog shows the IP address entered, with the username 'admin' and password 'password' filled in, or the camera is shown as successfully discovered/added in the interface.

task_completion.description: Rename the camera to 'Server Room Camera'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Camera Settings dialog (General tab) or the resource tree panel explicitly shows the camera named exactly 'Server Room Camera'.

task_completion.description: Enable continuous recording for the camera.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Camera Settings dialog is open to the 'Recording' tab, and recording is enabled (typically shown by a toggled switch and a schedule grid filled with the color indicating continuous recording).

task_completion.description: Configure the recording frame rate to 15 FPS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Camera Settings 'Recording' tab, the FPS (Frames Per Second) setting is explicitly set to 15.

integrity.description: The agent used the Nx Witness graphical user interface to add and configure the camera.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Nx Witness desktop client (dialogs, settings menus) rather than terminal commands or scripts modifying configuration files directly.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine Nx Witness application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI elements match a real video management system (Nx Witness/NetworkOptix) and are not just text files or mocked interfaces.

integrity.description: The agent actually opened the specified inventory file rather than using hardcoded knowledge.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is at least one screenshot showing the file '/home/ga/Documents/network_inventory.txt' being accessed or read.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target camera name: 'Server Room Camera'. Inventory file path: '/home/ga/Documents/network_inventory.txt'. Required FPS: 15. Credentials: username 'admin', password 'password'. The task is performed in the Nx Witness (NetworkOptix) client.
